binaries.title: csvflt.sys (Cluster Shared Volumes Filter) - TOCTOU Race / Use-After-Free on global EncryptionManager pointer

changed_functions.name: CsvCamSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C0039E30)
key_changes: UNPATCHED: Global pointer EncryptionManager (data_1c002f518) is read (0x1c0039e61) and dereferenced (fields +0x20 at 0x1c0039e70, +0x18 at 0x1c0039e77) WITHOUT holding any synchronization lock, while a concurrent execution path frees the old object (Encryption::scalar_deleting_destructor (sub_1c0027c28) -> ExFreePoolWithTag) and replaces the global under ExAcquireResourceExclusiveLite at 0x1c0039f54-0x1c0039f89. This creates a TOCTOU window where the pointer can be freed between read and dereference.

UNPATCHED: Second global pointer ReplayCacheManagerObject (data_1c002f508) is read under ExAcquireResourceSharedLite (0x1c0039fb2), but the lock is released (0x1c003a04a) before the pointer is used in a comparison-free branch — though this path is less critical since the shared lock covers the read.

PATCHED: The read of the FIRST global (EncryptionManager, renamed data_1c002f0c0) is now wrapped in RWLock::AcquireShared (sub_1c0027d70)(SigningLock (data_1c002f560)) / RWLock::ReleaseShared (sub_1c00289ec)(SigningLock (data_1c002f560)), closing the TOCTOU window. This acquire is unconditional (no feature gate). The SECOND global (ReplayCacheManagerObject, renamed data_1c002f0b8) was already read under a shared lock (ExAcquireResourceSharedLite) in the unpatched build; the patch only re-expresses that same shared acquire through the RWLock wrapper, which is a refactor, not a security change.

PATCHED: The guard was recompiled from 'test eax,eax; je' to 'cmp dword [rcx+0xd4], ebp; jbe' (ebp=0). Because jbe against zero is an unsigned below-or-equal-zero test, both forms branch away only when the length is exactly zero; this is an equivalent non-zero check (codegen difference), NOT a stricter bound.

The function Encryption::Initialize (sub_1c0028308) (unpatched copy-init helper) was replaced by Encryption::Initialize (sub_1c002833c) in the patched version — likely the same logic recompiled; no behavioral security impact.
changed_functions.security_notes: UNPATCHED flaw: CsvCamSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C0039E30) reads the global EncryptionManager (data_1c002f518) pointer (an Encryption/signing context object, not a reparse cache) without any lock at 0x1c0039e61, then dereferences it at 0x1c0039e70 (reading +0x20) and 0x1c0039e77 (reading +0x18). Meanwhile, a concurrent invocation of the same function (or the second global update path) can acquire the exclusive SigningLock (data_1c002f530) via ExAcquireResourceExclusiveLite at 0x1c0039f54, call Encryption::scalar_deleting_destructor (sub_1c0027c28) to free the old object, and overwrite EncryptionManager (data_1c002f518) with a newly allocated object at 0x1c0039f90. The first thread then dereferences the now-freed pointer -> classic Use-After-Free (CWE-416). The patch wraps the read-deref sequence in RWLock::AcquireShared (sub_1c0027d70) / RWLock::ReleaseShared (sub_1c00289ec) on the SAME SigningLock RWLock that the free+replace path holds exclusively (SigningLock (data_1c002f530) unpatched, relocated to SigningLock (data_1c002f560) patched): readers now take it in shared mode while the writer takes it exclusive, so the pointer cannot be freed during use. This is a race condition triggerable from a process that can open the \Csv2_FilterPort minifilter communication port (the port is created with a NULL SecurityDescriptor, so the effective access is whatever default the filter manager applies). Only the first global (EncryptionManager) was read unlocked in the unpatched build; the second global was already shared-locked.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED vulnerable block (no lock around global read+deref):
0x1c0039e59: test    eax, eax
0x1c0039e5b: je      0x1c0039fb2
0x1c0039e61: mov     rdx, qword [rel 0x1c002f518]   ; READ GLOBAL PTR - NO LOCK
0x1c0039e68: mov     r14b, r15b
0x1c0039e6b: test    rdx, rdx
0x1c0039e6e: je      0x1c0039ee7
0x1c0039e70: mov     ecx, dword [rdx+0x20]          ; DEREF freed ptr - UAF!
0x1c0039e73: cmp     eax, ecx
0x1c0039e75: jne     0x1c0039ebe
0x1c0039e77: mov     rdx, qword [rdx+0x18]          ; DEREF freed ptr - UAF!
0x1c0039e7e: lea     rcx, [rsi+0xd8]
0x1c0039e85: call    0x1c0008940

--- Concurrent free path (under ExAcquireResourceExclusiveLite) ---
0x1c0039f54: mov     rbx, qword [rel 0x1c002f530]   ; ERESOURCE
0x1c0039f5b: call    qword [rel 0x1c0032568]        ; KeEnterCriticalRegion
0x1c0039f67: mov     rcx, qword [rbx]
0x1c0039f6a: mov     dl, 0x1
0x1c0039f6c: call    qword [rel 0x1c00322c8]        ; ExAcquireResourceExclusiveLite
0x1c0039f78: mov     rcx, qword [rel 0x1c002f518]   ; read old ptr
0x1c0039f7f: test    rcx, rcx
0x1c0039f82: je      0x1c0039f89
0x1c0039f84: call    0x1c0027c28                    ; FREE old object (ExFreePoolWithTag)
0x1c0039f89: mov     rcx, qword [rel 0x1c002f530]
0x1c0039f90: mov     qword [rel 0x1c002f518], rdi   ; replace global with new alloc
0x1c0039f97: mov     rcx, qword [rcx]
0x1c0039f9a: call    qword [rel 0x1c00322e8]        ; ExReleaseResourceLite
0x1c0039fa6: call    qword [rel 0x1c0032560]        ; KeLeaveCriticalRegion

PATCHED: same block now wrapped in the shared lock:
0x1c0039e53: cmp     dword [rcx+0xd4], ebp          ; ebp=0; equivalent non-zero guard (jbe against 0 == len==0)
0x1c0039e59: jbe     0x1c0039fd2
0x1c0039e5f: mov     rcx, qword [rel SigningLock]   ; SigningLock (data_1c002f560)
0x1c0039e69: call    0x1c0027d70                    ; ACQUIRE SHARED (RWLock::AcquireShared)
0x1c0039e6e: mov     rdx, qword [rel EncryptionManager] ; read global UNDER LOCK (data_1c002f0c0)
0x1c0039e75: test    rdx, rdx
0x1c0039e78: je      0x1c0039ef2
0x1c0039e7a: mov     eax, dword [rdx+0x20]          ; safe deref under lock
0x1c0039e7d: cmp     dword [rsi+0xd4], eax
0x1c0039e83: jne     0x1c0039ec9
0x1c0039e85: mov     rdx, qword [rdx+0x18]
0x1c0039e89: lea     rcx, [rsi+0xd8]
0x1c0039e90: mov     r8d, eax
0x1c0039e93: call    0x1c0008940                    ; memcmp
...
0x1c0039ef2: mov     rcx, qword [rel SigningLock]
0x1c0039ef9: call    0x1c00289ec                    ; RELEASE SHARED (RWLock::ReleaseShared)

security_findings.entry_point: Minifilter communication port \Csv2_FilterPort (FltCreateCommunicationPort registered in DriverEntry DriverEntry (sub_1c003b078)), message handler CsvMessage (sub_1c0038460) with command code 1
call_chain: DriverEntry (sub_1c003b078) (DriverEntry) registers FltCreateCommunicationPort with message callback CsvMessage (sub_1c0038460)
CsvMessage (sub_1c0038460) (minifilter message dispatcher) copies input to a pool buffer, reads first DWORD as command code, if ==1 and input size>=0xDC calls CsvCamSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C0039E30)(P)
CsvCamSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C0039E30) reads global EncryptionManager (data_1c002f518) WITHOUT lock (0x1c0039e61), dereferences it (0x1c0039e70/0x1c0039e77)
CONCURRENT second invocation frees the object (ExFreePoolWithTag via Encryption::scalar_deleting_destructor (sub_1c0027c28) at 0x1c0039f84) under exclusive lock at 0x1c0039f54
First thread dereferences freed pointer -> UAF
security_findings.description: The CSV filter minifilter driver csvflt.sys maintains a global EncryptionManager (data_1c002f518) pointer (an Encryption/signing context object) that points to a PagedPool allocation (0x28 bytes, tag 'CVCA'). In the unpatched function CsvCamSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C0039E30), this pointer is read at 0x1c0039e61 and dereferenced at 0x1c0039e70 (field +0x20) and 0x1c0039e77 (field +0x18) WITHOUT holding ANY synchronization primitive. However, elsewhere in the SAME function (reached via the label_1c0039ef5 path when the cache is stale), the code acquires an ERESOURCE exclusive lock (ExAcquireResourceExclusiveLite at 0x1c0039f54 via the SigningLock (data_1c002f530) global), frees the old object via Encryption::scalar_deleting_destructor (sub_1c0027c28) -> ExFreePoolWithTag (0x1c0039f84), and replaces the global pointer with a freshly allocated object (0x1c0039f90). If two threads simultaneously invoke this function (e.g., two concurrent FilterSendMessage calls with command code 1), Thread A can read the pointer at 0x1c0039e61, then Thread B frees it and replaces it, then Thread A dereferences the now-dangling pointer. The patched version wraps the read of the first global in RWLock::AcquireShared (sub_1c0027d70) (acquire) / RWLock::ReleaseShared (sub_1c00289ec) (release) lock calls on SigningLock (data_1c002f560), preventing the free from occurring during the read-deref window. The guard was recompiled from 'test eax,eax; je' to 'cmp [rcx+0xd4], ebp; jbe' (ebp=0), which branches away only when the length is zero; it is an equivalent non-zero check, not a stricter bound.
security_findings.trigger_conditions:
1. Open a handle to the CSV filter minifilter port using FilterConnectCommunicationPort(L"\\Csv2_FilterPort", ...). The connection-notify callback (CsvConnect (sub_1c0038160)) accepts connections where the first DWORD of the connect context is 1 or 2 (client type), with context size >= 8 bytes.
2. Craft a message buffer of at least 0xDC bytes where the first DWORD (offset 0) = 1 (command code routing to CsvCamSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C0039E30)), offset +0xcc = a 32-bit 'connection ID' value, offset +0xd0 = a 32-bit 'sequence/flags' value, and offset +0xd4 = a non-zero 32-bit 'length' value.
3. Send two or more such messages CONCURRENTLY from different threads using FilterSendMessage to create the race window. Thread A must be in the unlocked read-deref path (0x1c0039e61-0x1c0039e85) while Thread B executes the free+replace path (0x1c0039f54-0x1c0039f90). The race is won when Thread A reads EncryptionManager (data_1c002f518) before Thread B frees it but dereferences it after Thread B frees it.
4. The free path is reached when the cached object's field +0x20 (length) differs from the incoming arg1+0xd4 value, OR when the cache comparison (memcmp (sub_1c0008940)) fails — i.e., sending a message whose +0xd4 length does not match the currently cached length forces allocation of a new cache entry and freeing of the old one.
security_findings.primitive: Use-After-Free: dereferencing a freed PagedPool allocation (0x28 bytes, tag 'CVCA'). After the object is freed, the dangling pointer is dereferenced at +0x20 (32-bit length, compared against arg1+0xd4) and +0x18 (passed as the Buf2/source pointer to memcmp (sub_1c0008940), compared against arg1+0xd8). The memcmp result is used only as a match/no-match boolean (it clears the function's ok flag and selects a WPP trace branch); the bytes it reads are never returned to the caller, so this is NOT a data-return/info-leak channel. The demonstrable impact from the binaries is kernel memory corruption / denial of service (bugcheck on the dangling read). A controlled-read info-leak, KASLR defeat, token theft, or code-execution chain is NOT evidenced by these binaries and would require additional primitives (reclamation and control) not present here.

CsvCamSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C0039E30) (UNPATCHED, csvflt_unpatched.sys) — vulnerable function full assembly:

0x1c0039e30: mov     qword [rsp+0x8], rbx

0x1c0039e3a: mov     qword [rsp+0x18], rsi
0x1c0039e3f: push    rdi

0x1c0039e42: push    r15

0x1c0039e4e: xor     ebp, ebp
0x1c0039e50: xor     r14b, r14b

0x1c0039e5b: je      0x1c0039fb2

0x1c0039e61: mov     rdx, qword [rel 0x1c002f518]   ; EncryptionManager (data_1c002f518) = cached Encryption/signing ctx ptr

0x1c0039eb6: xor     r14b, r14b

0x1c0039f0c: and     qword [rax+0x8], rbp

0x1c0039fe2: mov     r9d, dword [rax]               ; deref
0x1c0039fe5: cmp     r9d, dword [rsi+0xcc]          ; compare connection ID
0x1c0039fec: jne     0x1c003a021
; ... (telemetry / second global swap path follows) ...

breakpoints.why: Entry to the vulnerable function. rcx (arg1) points to the user-controlled message buffer containing command code at +0x0, connection ID at +0xcc, flags at +0xd0, and length at +0xd4. Set a conditional breakpoint to only break when [rcx+0xd4] != 0 to hit the vulnerable path.

The UNLOCKED read of the global pointer EncryptionManager (data_1c002f518) (mov rdx, [rel 0x1c002f518]). This is the start of the TOCTOU race window. rdx now holds the cached Encryption/signing context pointer that may be freed by a concurrent thread. Step over the next few instructions to reach the deref at +0x40.

The UAF dereference: mov ecx, dword [rdx+0x20]. If rdx has been freed by a concurrent thread between 0x1c0039e61 and here, this reads freed pool memory. Set a breakpoint on ExFreePoolWithTag (0x1c0027c3b) in parallel to observe the free racing with this deref. Also break at +0x47 (0x1c0039e77: mov rdx, [rdx+0x18]) for the second deref.

Entry to the free+replace path: acquires ERESOURCE exclusive lock at SigningLock (data_1c002f530). The actual free happens at +0x154 (0x1c0039f84: call Encryption::scalar_deleting_destructor (sub_1c0027c28)). This is the winning thread in the race — it frees the object that the losing thread will deref.
The actual ExFreePoolWithTag call that frees the old cached object. In the race, this free must complete before the losing thread reaches the deref at 0x1c0039e70/0x1c0039e77.

watch: [rel 0x1c002f518] (EncryptionManager (data_1c002f518)) — the global cached Encryption/signing context pointer being raced on. Watch for it changing between the read at 0x1c0039e61 and the deref at 0x1c0039e70.
rdx at 0x1c0039e61 — holds the value read from EncryptionManager (data_1c002f518). This is the pointer that becomes dangling.
rcx (arg1) — the user message buffer; [rcx+0xd4] is the length field that must be non-zero; [rcx+0xcc] is connection ID; [rcx+0xd0] is flags/sequence.
rdi at 0x1c0039f89 — the newly allocated replacement object written to EncryptionManager (data_1c002f518).
Pool tag 'CVCA' (0x41435643) — the ExAllocatePoolWithTag/ExFreePoolWithTag tag for the 0x28-byte cached object. Use '!poolfind CVCA' or 'poolmon' to track these allocations.
key_offsets: 0x1c0039e48: mov eax, [rcx+0xd4] — reads attacker-controlled length from message buffer
0x1c0039e61: mov rdx, [rel 0x1c002f518] — UNLOCKED global ptr read (TOCTOU window start)
0x1c0039e70: mov ecx, [rdx+0x20] — UAF deref #1 (reads 'length' field from freed 0x28-byte object)
0x1c0039e77: mov rdx, [rdx+0x18] — UAF deref #2 (reads 'data pointer' field from freed object)
0x1c0039f54: ERESOURCE exclusive lock acquire (SigningLock (data_1c002f530)) — the lock the vulnerable read SHOULD have held
0x1c0039f84: call Encryption::scalar_deleting_destructor (sub_1c0027c28) — ExFreePoolWithTag frees the old cached object (the winning race thread)
0x1c0039f90: mov [rel 0x1c002f518], rdi — replaces global with new allocation
0x1c0039f2e-0x1c0039f3f: Encryption::Initialize (sub_1c0028308) call — initializes new 0x28-byte object from user message data at arg1+0xd8
debugging_aids.trigger_setup:
1. The CSV filter driver (csvflt.sys) must be loaded — it is present on Windows Server Failover Clustering nodes with Cluster Shared Volumes enabled. On a cluster node, the minifilter port \Csv2_FilterPort is created by FltCreateCommunicationPort in DriverEntry (DriverEntry (sub_1c003b078) at 0x1c003b5c3), with a NULL SecurityDescriptor in the OBJECT_ATTRIBUTES.
2. Use FilterConnectCommunicationPort to connect to \Csv2_FilterPort with a connect context whose first DWORD is 1 or 2 (client type) and size >= 8 bytes. The connection-notify callback CsvConnect (sub_1c0038160) validates this.
3. Create TWO threads, each with an open handle to the port. Each thread calls FilterSendMessage with a buffer of >= 0xDC bytes: DWORD[0]=1 (command code), DWORD[0xcc/4]=connection_id, DWORD[0xd0/4]=sequence, DWORD[0xd4/4]=non_zero_length. Alternate the length value between messages so that the cache comparison (field +0x20 vs arg1+0xd4) fails, forcing the alloc+free+replace path.
4. Thread A sends a message that hits the read+deref path (cache exists, length matches). Thread B sends a message that hits the alloc+free+replace path (length mismatch forces reallocation). The race window is between Thread A's read at 0x1c0039e61 and deref at 0x1c0039e70/0x1c0039e77.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: If the race is won: Thread A dereferences freed pool memory at 0x1c0039e70 or 0x1c0039e77. With Special Pool enabled on csvflt.sys, the dangling read faults on the freed guard page — BUGCHECK 0x50 (PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA) with the faulting IP at csvflt+0x39e70. Without Special Pool, the deref reads stale or recycled pool data; if the page was fully released it may still fault with 0x50. Use '!pool rdx' at the deref breakpoint to see whether the pool block is marked Free.
debugging_aids.notes:
Key struct layout of the 0x28-byte cached object (tag 'CVCA', PagedPool):
  +0x00: field zeroed on alloc (likely refcount/state)
  +0x08: zeroed on alloc
  +0x10: zeroed on alloc
  +0x18: data pointer (passed to memcmp (sub_1c0008940) as source) — UAF deref target #2
  +0x20: 32-bit length (compared against arg1+0xd4) — UAF deref target #1

The message buffer (arg1, command code 1, min size 0xDC) layout:
  +0x00: command code (must be 1)
  +0xcc: 32-bit connection ID
  +0xd0: 32-bit sequence/flags
  +0xd4: 32-bit data length (must be non-zero for the read+deref path)
  +0xd8: start of inline data (source for Encryption::Initialize (sub_1c0028308) copy into the cached object)

The second global ReplayCacheManagerObject (data_1c002f508) (0x1010-byte objects, tag 'CVCA') is ALREADY read under ExAcquireResourceSharedLite at 0x1c0039fca in the unpatched build, so its read was not the vulnerable one. The patch only re-expresses that existing shared acquire through the RWLock::AcquireShared (sub_1c0027d70)/RWLock::ReleaseShared (sub_1c00289ec) wrapper (a refactor). Only the FIRST global (EncryptionManager) was read with no lock in the unpatched build.

Important: RWLock::AcquireShared (sub_1c0027d70) / RWLock::ReleaseShared (sub_1c00289ec) acquire the SAME SigningLock RWLock in SHARED mode; internally RWLock wraps the same ERESOURCE that the free+replace path takes in EXCLUSIVE mode (ExAcquireResourceExclusiveLite). No new lock object was introduced: SigningLock (data_1c002f530) in the unpatched build and SigningLock (data_1c002f560) in the patched build are the same global at relocated addresses across the two builds. The patch closes the race by making the first-global reader path take this reader-writer lock shared, matching the writer that already took it exclusive.

summary: The patch fixes a TOCTOU race condition (CWE-362) leading to a Use-After-Free (CWE-416) in csvflt.sys (Cluster Shared Volumes Filter driver). The vulnerable function CsvCamSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C0039E30) reads a global EncryptionManager (data_1c002f518) pointer (an Encryption/signing context object) and dereferences it (fields +0x20 and +0x18) WITHOUT holding any synchronization lock, while a concurrent invocation of the same function frees the old object via ExFreePoolWithTag and replaces the global under an ERESOURCE exclusive lock. An attacker able to open the \Csv2_FilterPort minifilter communication port (via FilterConnectCommunicationPort + FilterSendMessage) can trigger this race by sending two concurrent messages with command code 1 and mismatched length fields to force the free+replace path while the unlocked read executes. The fix wraps the first-global read in lock acquire (RWLock::AcquireShared (sub_1c0027d70)) / release (RWLock::ReleaseShared (sub_1c00289ec)) calls on the same SigningLock the writer holds exclusive, closing the race window unconditionally (no feature gate). The second global was already shared-locked and is only refactored. The demonstrable impact is kernel pool corruption / denial of service (bugcheck on the dangling read); the memcmp result is only a match/no-match boolean, so no info-leak or controlled-read primitive is evidenced by these binaries, and a full privilege-escalation chain is not demonstrated.
